description: Multi-tenant thread isolation via Gateway mapping layer + user info/logout UI in sidebar

userStories.title: Backend: Add bindUser endpoint to Gateway
userStories.description: As a backend developer, I want a POST /api/threads/bindUser endpoint that registers a user_id <-> thread_id mapping in the Store, so that thread ownership can be tracked.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/threads/bindUser endpoint exists on the threads router
Endpoint uses @require_auth decorator to extract user_id from JWT cookie
Mapping is written to Store with namespace ("thread_owners",), key=thread_id, value={"user_id": ..., "created_at": ...}
Endpoint is idempotent: if mapping already exists with same user_id, returns success
Returns {"success": true} on success
Returns 401 if no valid auth cookie
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Backend: Add listByUser endpoint to Gateway
userStories.description: As a backend developer, I want a GET /api/threads/listByUser endpoint that returns only threads owned by the current user, with metadata from the checkpointer.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/threads/listByUser endpoint exists on the threads router
Endpoint uses @require_auth decorator to extract user_id from JWT cookie
Queries Store namespace ("thread_owners",) and filters by user_id
For each owned thread_id, reads latest checkpoint from checkpointer to get title, updated_at, status
Returns Thread[] array compatible with LangGraph threads.search format (thread_id, status, created_at, updated_at, metadata, values with title)
Results sorted by updated_at descending
Returns empty array for users with no threads
Returns 401 if no valid auth cookie
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Backend: Add mapping cleanup to DELETE endpoint
userStories.description: As a backend developer, I want the existing DELETE /api/threads/{thread_id} endpoint to also delete the thread_owners mapping record and verify ownership.
acceptanceCriteria: DELETE /api/threads/{thread_id} now also calls store.adelete(("thread_owners",), thread_id)
Endpoint has @require_auth decorator added
Endpoint verifies the mapping's user_id matches the current user before deleting (returns 404 if mismatch or not found)
Mapping deletion is idempotent (no error if mapping doesn't exist)
Existing local thread data cleanup (filesystem, checkpointer, store threads namespace) still works
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Frontend: Switch useThreads to call Gateway listByUser
userStories.description: As a frontend developer, I want useThreads() to fetch the thread list from Gateway's /api/threads/listByUser instead of LangGraph's threads.search, so that only the current user's threads are shown.
acceptanceCriteria: useThreads() in frontend/src/core/threads/hooks.ts calls fetchWithAuth(getBackendBaseURL() + '/api/threads/listByUser') instead of apiClient.threads.search()
Response is parsed as AgentThread[] and returned to the component
TanStack Query queryKey remains ['threads', 'search'] so existing invalidateQueries calls still work
refetchOnWindowFocus remains false
Sidebar shows empty list for new users with no threads
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Frontend: Register thread ownership on creation
userStories.description: As a frontend developer, I want the useThreadStream onCreated callback to register the new thread with Gateway's bindUser endpoint, so that newly created threads appear in the user's list.
acceptanceCriteria: onCreated callback in useThreadStream (frontend/src/core/threads/hooks.ts) calls fetchWithAuth POST /api/threads/bindUser with {thread_id: meta.thread_id}
The bindUser call uses .catch(console.error) so failures don't block the conversation
Existing handleStreamStart and setOnStreamThreadId calls are preserved
After creating a thread and sending a message, the thread appears in the sidebar on refresh
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Frontend: Show user email in sidebar bottom
userStories.description: As a logged-in user, I want to see my email address displayed at the bottom of the sidebar so I know which account I'm using.
acceptanceCriteria: workspace-nav-menu.tsx imports and uses useAuth() hook to get user.email
When sidebar is expanded, user email is displayed above or near the 'Settings and More' trigger
Email is truncated with ellipsis if too long
When sidebar is collapsed, a circular avatar with the first letter of the email is shown
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Frontend: Add logout button to sidebar menu
userStories.description: As a logged-in user, I want a logout button in the sidebar dropdown menu so I can sign out of my account.
acceptanceCriteria: A 'Logout' / '退出登录' menu item is added to the DropdownMenu in workspace-nav-menu.tsx
It appears at the bottom of the menu, separated from other items by a DropdownMenuSeparator
It uses the LogOut icon from lucide-react
Clicking it calls useAuth().logout() then navigates to /login via router.push
After logout, accessing /workspace redirects to /login (AuthGuard)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Data cleanup: Clear historical thread data
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to clear existing thread data that has no user_id mapping, so we start fresh with the isolation system.
acceptanceCriteria: backend/.deer-flow/checkpoints.db, checkpoints.db-wal, checkpoints.db-shm are deleted if they exist
backend/.deer-flow/memory.json is deleted if it exists
backend/.langgraph_api/ directory is deleted if it exists
A shell script or documented commands exist for this cleanup
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is a one-time operation before deploying the isolation feature. Can be a simple script in scripts/ or documented in the PRD.
